# Lecturecast

Lecturecast is a commercial AgentMesh360 course-video product with a cloud
Director for structured creative decisions and local media production.

## Canonical URL

https://lecturecast.agentmesh360.com/

## Available Languages

- 中文 (zh-CN, default): https://lecturecast.agentmesh360.com/
- English (en): https://lecturecast.agentmesh360.com/en/
- 日本語 (ja): https://lecturecast.agentmesh360.com/ja/
- 한국어 (ko): https://lecturecast.agentmesh360.com/ko/

Non-Chinese pages are machine-assisted translations; the Chinese page is authoritative for any conflict.

## Product Summary

- Commercial access: a current paid AgentMesh360 account, universal API key, and
  sufficient shared credits are required before production begins.
- Director: required cloud access for native agent choice cards, a Creative
  Brief, and a signed declarative ProductionManifest.
- Input: a topic or idea for a course video.
- Output: Bilibili landscape video and Xiaohongshu portrait video.
- Workflow: script writing, voice generation, timeline alignment, Remotion rendering, ffmpeg subtitle burn-in, and cover generation.
- Cloud/local boundary: original media, voice, subtitles, editing, Remotion,
  ffmpeg, covers, and rendered outputs stay on the user's machine.
- Director data boundary: only a bounded source summary, stable choice IDs, the
  Brief, and client capabilities are sent to the cloud.
- Director access: available to a paid AgentMesh360 account using a universal
  AgentMesh360 API key. It reuses the active monthly pass and shared credits;
  there is no separate LectureCast pass. Choices and Brief creation are
  free; 10 credits are deducted only after the user confirms ProductionManifest
  generation.
- Availability: paid AgentMesh360 accounts with sufficient shared credits.
- License: Apache 2.0.
- Source code: https://github.com/jiyangnan/AgentMesh-Lecturecast

## Best Short Description

Lecturecast is an AgentMesh360 commercial course-video product: the required
Director adds structured cloud creative decisions and a signed ProductionManifest
while all media production and rendering stay on the user's machine.
